level_signals: 10+ years of product management experience required
Titled 'Group Product Manager' signaling senior IC leadership
JD explicitly calls this 'a high-impact IC leadership role for a seasoned payments expert'
Base salary range $243K–$287K consistent with senior/staff IC at Coinbase

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience, with deep expertise in payments infrastructure, payment processing, or fintech platforms.
Track record of owning and driving multi-year platform strategies that delivered measurable improvements in processing success rates, cost efficiency, or global payment coverage.

Deep technical fluency in payment systems architecture, processing flows, multi-partner routing, payment method economics, interchange optimization, and the regulatory landscape for payments across major markets.

Demonstrated ability to lead complex, cross-functional initiatives in highly regulated environments, driving alignment across engineering, finance, compliance, and business stakeholders, and articulating platform strategy and investment tradeoffs to executive leadership.
Utilizes generative AI responsibly, maintaining human oversight to deliver business-ready outputs and drive measurable improvements in workflow efficiency, cost, and quality.

scope.surface: Payments Core — fiat money movement infrastructure processing hundreds of billions of dollars annually across 15+ payment methods in 30+ countries
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B platform, crypto exchange / fintech
primary_metrics_inferred: Self-serve adoption by internal product groups
core_responsibilities: Own the end-to-end product roadmap for Payments Core, encompassing core payment processing, payment method integrations (ACH, cards, bank transfers), multi-partner infrastructure, and payment lifecycle management.
Drive optimization of payment processing success rates, least-cost routing, and transaction efficiency to directly improve Coinbase's revenue and customer experience across its global footprint.
Lead the strategy and execution for multi-partner payments infrastructure, enabling processing partner routing, redundancy, and failover to maximize reliability and cost efficiency.
Partner with Engineering to shape system architecture and technical investments that drive scalability, resilience, and adaptability across Payments Core's platform.
Build self-serve capabilities that enable Product Groups to leverage Payments Core without heavy integration overhead, unblocking growth across Coinbase's product surface.
Shape Payments Core's long-term strategy for emerging payment methods, real-time payment networks, and evolving regulatory landscapes.

compensation.equity_form: equity eligibility mentioned alongside bonus and benefits

green_flags: Equity and bonus eligibility mentioned in addition to base
Clear, specific product surface with quantified scale (hundreds of billions annually, 15+ payment methods, 30+ countries)
High-impact IC role with explicit ownership of multi-year strategy — not a support or execution-only role
Role sits within Platform product group — high organizational visibility
red_flags: Quarterly in-person 'surges' required — may be a constraint for candidates who cannot travel periodically
10+ years minimum with deep payments infrastructure expertise is a narrow talent pool requirement
No nice-to-haves or preferred qualifications listed — JD is entirely mandatory requirements, leaving no flexibility signals
Visa sponsorship and work authorization policies not stated — unclear for non-US applicants
AI-based initial screening interview disclosed — candidates should be aware of automated first-round process

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Group PM', comp range, and JD language 'high-impact IC leadership role')
scope.business_model (inferred from company context — crypto exchange / fintech platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as KPIs in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Base salary varies by location' disclaimer)
ambiguities: JD states Coinbase is 'remote-first, but not remote-only' and mentions quarterly in-person surges, but no specific city or office location is listed for this role. Treated as remote-us with periodic travel requirement.

The generative AI requirement ('Utilizes generative AI responsibly...') is listed under 'Required Skills and Experience' with no qualifier; treated as hard requirement, though it reads more like a company-wide behavioral expectation than a screening criterion.
